message:
feat(tui/components): add shared state rendering component (ntm-952.1.1)

Introduce a reusable RenderState component that provides consistent
empty/loading/error state rendering across all TUI surfaces. This
establishes a design-system foundation for premium UX consistency.

New component (state.go):
- StateKind enum: StateEmpty, StateLoading, StateError
- StateOptions struct: configurable icon, message, hint, width, alignment
- RenderState(): main renderer with intelligent defaults per state kind
- EmptyState(), LoadingState(), ErrorState(): convenience wrappers

Key behaviors:
- StateEmpty: italicized overlay text with info icon, "Nothing to show"
- StateLoading: subtext color with gear icon, "Loading…" default message
- StateError: red text with warning icon, supports hint line for remediation
- Width-aware truncation via layout.TruncateRunes
- Respects icon set (nerd/unicode/ascii) via icons.Current()
- Supports center/right alignment with proper width handling

Design rationale:
- Consolidates previously scattered inline lipgloss styles
- Ensures every panel/screen communicates status consistently
- Includes retry/refresh affordance pattern (hint parameter)
- Respects icon and width fallbacks for accessibility

Tests added:
- TestRenderState: empty, loading, error, truncation scenarios
- Verifies default messages, hint inclusion, ellipsis truncation

Part of the Premium UX Polish epic (ntm-952) to achieve Stripe-level
terminal UX through consistent information hierarchy and actionable copy.
